summary: Iteration 2 (logo upload, multi-room quotations, public link, AI endpoints) — 18/19 backend tests PASS. Frontend smoke confirms AI panel (3 buttons), public link panel (generate/copy/revoke), and public /q/:token page (no auth, hero with first name, itinerary, Confirmar y reservar). Public accept endpoint sets state=ganada and accepted_at. CRITICAL ROUTING BUG: /uploads/logos/* is NOT reachable via the K8s ingress (only /api/* is forwarded to backend), so logo URLs returned by the backend (e.g. /uploads/logos/{tenant}.png) render the React SPA index.html instead of the image. As a consequence, sidebar logo, PDF logo (works only because PDF generator reads from local filesystem), and public quotation page logo all appear broken in the browser (img.naturalWidth=0).

critical.issue: Static logo URL not reachable via public ingress. K8s/Cloudflare routing only forwards /api/* to backend; everything else (including /uploads) returns the React SPA HTML (200 text/html). Result: always loads index.html, never the actual image. Backend StaticFiles mount works correctly on localhost:8001 but is unreachable from the public URL.

ui_bugs.component: AppShell sidebar CompanyBrand + PublicQuotation hero + Settings preview
ui_bugs.issue: Logo tags after upload have naturalWidth=0 (broken image). Caused by the /uploads routing bug above. UI shows a tiny broken-image icon next to 'Aventúrate por Jalisco' in the sidebar.

action_items: FIX LOGO ROUTING: Either (a) move StaticFiles mount from app.mount('/uploads', ...) to app.mount('/api/uploads', ...) and update server.py to return logo_url='/api/uploads/logos/{file}', or (b) add a GET /api/companies/me/logo endpoint that streams the file bytes (and also a public /api/public/companies/{tenant_id}/logo for the public quotation page), or (c) configure the K8s ingress to forward /uploads/* to the backend service. Option (a) is the smallest change.

Once routing is fixed, also update Settings page logo preview, AppShell CompanyBrand, PublicQuotation hero, and pdf_generator.py if it references the URL (currently it reads the local file path directly so PDF is fine).

Optional: PDF generator should be resilient to a corrupt/unreadable logo file (currently it crashes with 500 if logo image bytes are malformed — PIL OSError 'broken data stream'). Wrap the Image flowable in a try/except and skip the logo on failure.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L688: app.mount('/uploads', StaticFiles(...)) sits outside the /api prefix → not reachable behind the ingress that only routes /api/*. This is the root cause of the broken logo.

pricing.py multi-room math verified numerically: 2 doble (4 pax) + 1 triple (3 pax) + 1 menor → subtotal = price_doble*4 + price_triple*3 + price_minor*1. Confirmed by test_multi_room_create_and_pricing.
server.py L549: secrets.token_urlsafe(18) generates 24-char URL-safe tokens (>=18 visually); public-link expiry checked against UTC, OK.

server.py L607: $set on 'public_link.accepted_at' uses dotted path — works because public_link doc already exists; would no-op if link was revoked first (defensive: not an issue here because revoke uses $unset).

ai_service.py is invoked via emergentintegrations; all three quotation AI endpoints (next-step, missing-fields, client-message) and chat-summary returned valid Spanish content within ~3-6s each. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY present in /app/backend/.env.
pdf_generator.py: when a corrupt PNG is on disk, doc.build() raises OSError → 500 to client. Should wrap logo Image in try/except.
Public endpoint GET /api/public/quotations/{token} correctly omits company.pricing_config (uses {'pricing_config': 0} projection) — good leak prevention.
Public accept endpoint flips state to 'ganada' unconditionally — even if quotation was previously 'perdida'. Probably intentional, but worth a guard in future.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (logo broken everywhere; multi-room builder UI confirmed in source but not navigated end-to-end via playwright due to multi-step wizard — feature exists at QuotationBuilder.jsx L226-247 with rooms-total testid)

seed_data_creation: Tests created ~5 extra quotations on admin tenant (COT-202601x) plus 1 logo file de7483e7-...png on filesystem. One existing quotation was set to state='ganada' via public accept flow (COT-2026018).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration2.py — 18/19 pass. The single failure (test_logo_static_serves) intentionally fails until the /uploads routing is fixed; once main agent moves the StaticFiles mount under /api/uploads and updates logo_url accordingly, re-run pytest and that test should pass (it already calls $BASE_URL{logo_url} which will naturally start with /api/uploads/ after the fix). Multi-room builder UI wasn't navigated end-to-end via playwright (the wizard required client selection before reaching step 2); source code (QuotationBuilder.jsx L226-247) confirms feature is implemented with data-testid='rooms-total'. AI endpoints take 3-6s each; use timeout>=20s in playwright when clicking those buttons.
rca of the issue: The Routiq deployment uses a Kubernetes ingress that forwards only paths matching /api/* to the FastAPI backend (port 8001); all other paths fall through to the React frontend (port 3000 / Express SPA). FastAPI server mounts StaticFiles at /uploads, which is correct internally but invisible to public traffic. When the company logo is uploaded, the backend stores logo_url='/uploads/logos/{tenant}.{ext}' and returns it to the SPA, which then renders . The browser request resolves through the ingress to the frontend, which returns its index.html with content-type text/html for any unmatched path, yielding an image element with naturalWidth=0 (broken). Reproduction: login as admin@aventurate.mx → Ajustes → upload any PNG → observe sidebar 'Aventúrate por Jalisco' with broken thumbnail; or curl -I https:///uploads/logos/anything.png returns 200 text/html. Mitigation: mount StaticFiles at /api/uploads (one-line change in server.py) and have the upload handler return logo_url='/api/uploads/logos/{file}'. The local filesystem path used by pdf_generator.py is unaffected.
